---
title: "story_sitrep"
---

[< Back to Reference](../reference.qmd) | [View source](https://codeberg.org/usrbinr/stwd/src/branch/main/R/stwd.R#L1154)

# `story_sitrep()`

Get a Situation Report for a Story Layout

## Usage

```r
story_sitrep(
  plot = NULL,
  title = NULL,
  subtitle = NULL,
  narrative = NULL,
  caption = NULL,
  narrative_position = "right",
  narrative_width = 0.35,
  title_size = 16,
  subtitle_size = 11,
  narrative_size = 10,
  caption_size = 9,
  title_height = 0.15,
  subtitle_height = 0.07,
  caption_height = 0.05,
  show_tips = TRUE
)
```

## Arguments

| Argument | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `plot` | The main ggplot visualization (or NULL to just see layout structure). |
| `title` | Optional title (text or ggplot). |
| `subtitle` | Optional subtitle (text or ggplot). |
| `narrative` | Optional narrative (text or ggplot). |
| `caption` | Optional caption (text or ggplot). |
| `narrative_position` | Position of narrative: "right", "left", "bottom". |
| `narrative_width` | Width proportion for narrative (0-1). |
| `title_size` | Size for title in pts. |
| `subtitle_size` | Size for subtitle in pts. |
| `narrative_size` | Size for narrative text in pts. |
| `caption_size` | Size for caption in pts. |
| `title_height` | Height proportion for title row. |
| `subtitle_height` | Height proportion for subtitle row. |
| `caption_height` | Height proportion for caption row. |
| `show_tips` | Logical. Show SWD-inspired tips? Default: TRUE. |

## Value

A `StorySitrep` S7 object with properties:

- @components: data.frame of component details

- @layout: list of layout proportions

- @diagram: ASCII representation of the layout

- @tips: character vector of SWD-inspired suggestions

## Description

Returns diagnostic information about a story layout composition,
including which components are present, their sizes, layout proportions,
and an ASCII diagram of the structure.

## Examples

```r
# Check layout before building
story_sitrep(
    title = "My Title",
    subtitle = "My Subtitle",
    narrative = "Some text",
    narrative_position = "right"
)
```
